And when you intentionally ignore the obvious, then it has to come back to some jealousy, or some envy, or just some straight BS. Now this man has every basketball record on the books, and if he ain't in your Top 5, then you're full of sh*t.'

item.title: Rich Paul: 10 to 12 teams have called to show interest in LeBron James
item.description: NBA on ESPN: Despite rumors circulating, Rich Paul tells @PatMcAfeeshow that he hasn't had a conversation with LeBron James about next season, but has received calls from "about 10-12 teams" interested 👀 "Believe nothing that's out there because I haven't had one conversation with him."

item.title: “I’d probably go with [LeBron James] because Bron — …
item.description: Shaquille O’Neal won four championships and an MVP Award over his 19 seasons in the NBA, and has since studied it closely as an analyst. So whom would he select if he were a general manager and could choose any player — past or present — with the No. 1 pick? “Me,” O’Neal told The Post. Excluding himself? “I’d probably go with [LeBron James] because Bron — and I know this is a Kobe [Bryant] comparison — Bron had it when he came in,” O’Neal told The Post. “It took Kobe two or three years to ge

item.title: Spurs and Oklahoma City Thunder open as co-favorites …
item.description: The San Antonio Spurs and Oklahoma City Thunder open as co-favorites to win the 2027 NBA Finals at +250, followed by the Boston Celtics at +550. The New York Knicks, despite winning the championship on Saturday, have the fourth best odds at +700. The next block of teams have significantly longer odds beginning with the Indiana Pacers and Denver Nuggets at +2800, and the Minnesota Timbewolves, Los Angeles Lakers, Detroit Pistons and Cleveland Cavaliers at +3000.

item.title: Victor Wembanyama on losing NBA Finals: 'It's extremely painful, but I'm not running away from that'
item.description: NBA Base: Victor Wembanyama when asked about greats like Michael Jordan and LeBron James taking years to win their first ring: "It’s extremely painful. It’s painful, but I’m not running away from that. I’m using that to fuel me. I'm sure all these guys you named, they're not satisfied with being eliminated in earlier rounds or not making the playoffs, and I'm not satisfied with not winning."

item.title: LeBron James remains the youngest NBA player ever to …
item.description: Jorge Sierra: Jalen Brunson's 45-point game means Victor Wembanyama ended up not leading the playoffs in scoring. So LeBron James remains the youngest NBA player ever to top the postseason in points.

item.title: So when he went to replace Jackson in 2019, he kept …
item.description: Remember LeBron James' "Decision" back in 2010? That was Rose, James' agent at the time, and Worldwide Wes. Carmelo Anthony's trade from the Denver Nuggets to the Knicks in 2011? Leon and Wes. Dolan had a good relationship with both men during Anthony's seven seasons in New York and admired the loyalty they seemed to have with clients. Dolan is a lot of things. Irascible. Unpredictable. Meddlesome. But he's also loyal. So when he went to replace Jackson in 2019, he kept coming back to the idea o

item.title: Lakers expected to re-sign Austin Reaves
item.description: The Lakers are widely expected to re-sign Reaves, whose fondness for Lakerland as well as his blossoming backcourt partnership alongside Luka Dončić are regarded as strong lures that suggest the sides will ultimately come to terms on a new pact.

item.title: Isiah Thomas on LeBron James' legacy: 'He got every basketball record on the books and he ain't in your top five? You full of sh*t'
item.description: Isiah Thomas: "I'm calling it out, guys are jealous, they get envious. You bring them on the show, then they want to talk about what they did or what somebody else did, and then they intentionally ignore the obvious. And when you intentionally ignore the obvious, then that has to come back to some jealousy or envy or just some straight-up BS. Now this man got every basketball record on the books and he ain't in your top five, you full of shit."

item.title: Lakers giving LeBron James space to decide his future
item.description: Rob Pelinka, the Lakers’ president of basketball operations and general manager, said that the organization wanted to give James the space and time necessary to figure out whatever he plans to do next. Team sources have said that stance has not changed. James’ agent, Rich Paul, told Pat McAfee Friday that he hadn’t even discussed options for next season with James (though he mentioned interest from a third of the league should James decide to continue playing).

item.title: Utah Jazz will match any offer sheet Walker Kessler …
item.description: The expectation around the league is that the Utah Jazz will match any offer sheet Walker Kessler receives, and executives hold a similar view regarding Detroit and Jalen Duren. Another restricted free agent center at least worth monitoring is Mark Williams in Phoenix (yeah, the guy the Lakers traded for before rescinding the deal). But it’s only logical to wonder why the Suns would give up assets to acquire Williams only to let him walk a season later.

item.title: Immediately following the Knicks’ win over the Spurs …
item.description: Sales of Knicks championship gear has already more than doubled that of Fanatics’ previous best-selling NBA Finals champion: the LeBron James-led Los Angeles Lakers in 2020. Immediately following the Knicks’ win over the Spurs on Saturday night, Fanatics released more than 300 championship-branded products. The company took in more than 8,000 orders per minute after the Knicks’ 4-1 series win, also a record.

item.title: One thing that's repeated to me in my talks with …
item.description: Dan Woike: One thing that's repeated to me in my talks with Lakers/NBA sources: Not uncommon to be unsure about what's going to present itself at this time of year. Dominoes need to fall before a clear picture presents itself for teams. Like the Lakers have options - that's good! It also means that there isn't a singular totally obvious option. It doesn’t mean there's no "plan." It means you have to build multiple ones.

item.title: 2025-26 Star Participation %
item.description: Tom Haberstroh: PLAYOFF UPDATE: It is true that stars play through soreness/injuries more in the playoffs. This postseason, stars played 88.9% of its games, way up from reg season. Most games missed: Luka, KD, JDub. 2025-26 Star Participation % REG SEASON ... 66.2% PLAYOFFS ......... 88.7%

item.title: "When you look at the playoffs, they need a combo big …
item.description: According to ESPN's Bobby Marks, the Spurs have the financial flexibility to be active in free agency and could target a stretch forward capable of complementing Wembanyama. "The Spurs have the flexibility to go out and do some free-agency shopping," Marks said during ESPN's NBA Today. "When you look at the playoffs, they need a combo big man who can complement Victor [Wembanyama]. When you look at a stretch 4, a player like Rui Hachimura from the Lakers, you pair him up with a big who can shoot

item.title: Many rival teams believe this is the best pathway …
item.description: If James remains with the Lakers, they'll need to come to a number that significantly reduces his salary, which was over $50 million last season, and still leaves space to add a quality player. James' willingness to lower his salary could depend on what the Lakers are able to deliver with the savings. But many rival teams believe this is the best pathway forward for both the Lakers and James and, in the end, both will get there even if there are some bruises from the process.

item.title: In his 23rd NBA season, LeBron had the most viewed NBA …
item.description: Basketball Reference: Another season on the throne for King James 👑 In his 23rd NBA season, LeBron had the most viewed NBA player page on Basketball Reference! 🥇

item.title: Jaxson Hayes is now Slovenian
item.description: Iztok Franko: Jaxson Hayes received a Slovenian passport at Slovenian Embassy and n Washington

item.title: Luka Doncic compares Real Madrid with Lakers: 'At both clubs, the expectation is always to win'
item.description: Luka Dončić admitted it always feels special to return to Madrid. “It’s always great to come back here,” he said. “I’ve returned many times, and it’s always beautiful to be in Madrid.” Having experienced the pressure of playing for Real Madrid, Dončić sees clear similarities with life at the Lakers. Although the Lakers reached the playoffs, Dončić had little interest in celebrating the team’s progress. “It’s pretty similar,” he said. “At both clubs, the expectation is always to win. If you

item.title: Dončić insisted individual awards remain secondary. …
item.description: Despite being considered one of the league’s perennial MVP candidates, Dončić insisted individual awards remain secondary. “The media decides those awards,” he said. “Of course I want to win every trophy, but the most important thing is winning the championship.” With emerging contenders across the NBA, including San Antonio, Oklahoma City, New York and Boston, Dončić believes the Western Conference remains wide open. “There are so many great teams,” he said. “Finishing third or fourth in th

item.title: Dončić also praised former teammate Jalen Brunson, …
item.description: Dončić also praised former teammate Jalen Brunson, saying he had congratulated him after his strong postseason and also spoke about the possibility of what might have been if they both had stayed in Dallas. “He’s played really well. I sent him a message, and he has every reason to be happy. We’ve both improved a lot since then. We’re not the same players we used to be. It would have been very good, for sure.”

item.title: Windhorst: Lakers, LeBron James are in active negotiations on a new deal
item.description: ESPN Cleveland: "I think the focus right now is making a deal with the Lakers. Right now he's allowed to negotiate with the Lakers and I believe they are negotiating, I believe they're going back and forth," - @WindhorstESPN on LeBron's future.

item.title: Legendary Lakers PA announcer Lawrence Tanter is retiring
item.description: Dan Woike: Lakers longtime legendary PA announcer Lawrence Tanter is retiring. Will remain in organization as an advisor. Tanter suffered a health incident last March and was unable to work the remainder of the season.

item.title: Tanter suffered a stroke in March, sources told ESPN, …
item.description: Tanter suffered a stroke in March, sources told ESPN, causing him to miss the Lakers' last six home games of the regular season, plus the playoffs. Tanter continues to rehab from the health setback, sources said. Jason Barquero, the P.A. announcer for the Lakers' G League affiliate, finished the season in Tanter's absence.

item.title: That. I don't think you could have five greater …
item.description: Cedric Maxwell: "LeBron James is one of my favorite players because the longevity and what he's done. He is one of the top players of all-time. So I don't think that I could go away from that. I don't think you could have five greater players in the NBA than LeBron James and what he's done for the league and how he's really handled himself. A guy who has been no trouble, won championships, and you think about the personnel that have been around him, and other personnel that have been around him,

item.title: “He showed that star potential when Luka [Doncic] got …
item.description: “He showed that star potential when Luka [Doncic] got hurt, and he won those games against Utah for us, but we were trying to get a star to put next to Luka, and JB’s star had not risen yet,” Cuban said.

item.title: Iman Shumpert: LeBron James is the ChatGPT of the NBA
item.description: James drew an eye-opening comparison to ChatGPT from former Cleveland Cavaliers teammate Iman Shumpert, the 3-and-D guard with whom he won a title back in 2016. “He is ChatGPT of the NBA, bro. This the best way I can describe [him]. He's ChatGPT. That's Bron. You can ask him anything, he know. He know the assistant coaches, he know the player development. He is really programmed for this,” Shumpert said in an appearance on the Club Shay Shay podcast, via @Ankara_inc on X (formerly Twitter).

item.title: Warriors a willing suitor for LeBron James if negotiations with Lakers break down
item.description: If negotiations between James and the Los Angeles Lakers break down -- and if James goes searching for a new home for his 24th NBA season -- the Warriors are a willing suitor, team sources said. But openness does not equal anticipation. All the intel that Warriors decision-makers have gathered continues to point toward James' return to the Lakers, team sources said, and they are currently plotting their summer under that premise.

item.title: Austin Reaves to decline player option, is projected to earn $40 million annually in free agency
item.description: Austin Reaves will decline his $14.9 million player option for the 2026-27 season and enter unrestricted free agency as one of the top players on the market this summer. Declining his player option was always going to be a formality since Reaves has put himself in a position to earn a projected salary of over $40 million per season. Reaves can sign for up to a five-year, projected $239 million deal with the Lakers or a four-year, $177 million deal with another team.

item.title: If Lakers low-ball Austin Reaves he'll consider other offers
item.description: Ultimately, there’s a strong belief within league circles that if the Lakers offer Austin Reaves close to or the full max, he’ll stay in Los Angeles. However, if the Lakers submit a low-ball offer, Reaves will consider other offers, and the possibility of a sign-and-trade could also emerge. Brooklyn has Reaves on their radar and has the clearest path to offering a max contract outright in free agency with cap space.

item.title: “I think mainly shooters and a big man who can run the …
item.description: Speaking with Gigantes del Basket during the Jordan Brand’s ‘The One’ Madrid stop, Doncic was asked what specific player qualities he requires to perform at his absolute ceiling, and outlined a clear, two-pronged demand. Note: These are approximate translations from Spanish. “I think mainly shooters and a big man who can run the pick-and-roll and jump so I can pass to him,” Doncic told the interviewers. “If I have shooters, they won’t double me as much and it helps me out. I think I always need

item.title: The Lakers hosted a workout on Monday with the …
item.description: Cody Taylor: Sources: The Lakers hosted a workout on Monday with the following prospects: 🏀 Oscar Cluff, Purdue 🏀 Josh Dix, Creighton 🏀 Roddy Gayle Jr., Michigan 🏀 Nate Johnson, Kansas State 🏀 Tarris Reed Jr., UConn 🏀 Kowacie Reeves Jr., Georgia Tech

item.title: Kevin Love to reunite with LeBron James on the Lakers?
item.description: NBA on ESPN: "I'm hearing that [LeBron's] likely coming back [to the Lakers]. ... Kevin Love could likely be joining the Lakers too." @MarcJSpears gives some insight on what could be next for LeBron 🏀

item.title: "Bron kind of holds you accountable like yeah, I'm …
item.description: Fullcourtpass: Iman Shumpert says LeBron James will throw a turnover on purpose to prove you were out of position "Bron kind of holds you accountable like yeah, I'm going to throw a turnover if you're not in the spot I told you to be" (Via @OutTheMudTL, h/t @0xarslan)

item.title: The Warriors' pitch, if the door cracks open, would be …
item.description: The Warriors' pitch, if the door cracks open, would be simple. They can clear room for the full $15.1 million nontaxpayer midlevel -- a team-friendly, low-risk bargain with on-court and off-court financial upside. LeBron James is an obvious top free agent target at that price point. The Warriors could theoretically tack on a second season player option and get Stephen Curry in on the recruitment process, if required. But they haven't knocked on that door because they've been given any indication

item.title: Doncic: Well, I think above all, shooters and a center …
item.description: Q. What qualities do the players around you need to have for you to be at your best? Luka Doncic: Well, I think above all, shooters and a center who can run the pick and roll and jump in so I can pass to him. And if I have shooters, they can't double-team me as much, so it helps me a lot.

item.title: Several league sources told ESPN they believe Rui …
item.description: Several league sources told ESPN they believe Rui Hachimura could be the odd man out and that he might need to explore the market to find a desired deal. However, if the Lakers decide to operate as an over-the-cap team, they would be able to retain all of their own free agents for whom they have Bird rights -- including accommodating Hachimura -- and also have the $15.1 million non-taxpayer midlevel exception, according to ESPN NBA front office insider Bobby Marks, to pursue outside free agents.

item.title: Lakers are interested in retaining Marcus Smart and …
item.description: The Lakers are interested in retaining Marcus Smart and sharpshooter Luke Kennard, sources told ESPN. Smart, who was recruited to L.A. by Doncic and has until June 29 to exercise the final year of his contract at $5.4 million, enjoyed his role with the Lakers and is hoping for a new contract and a raise, sources said. Kennard, for his part, was on an $11 million expiring contract and has interest in making L.A. his long-term home after previously playing 2½ seasons with the LA Clippers.

item.title: "Luka's first and foremost desire is an A-list …
item.description: While the 27-year-old Luka Doncic is preparing for another season squarely in his prime, he has made one roster preference clear to Pelinka and Redick, sources said. It's the same one he made in the days after he first arrived in Los Angeles 16 months ago. "Luka's first and foremost desire is an A-list center," the source said.

item.title: Two of them, Jalen Duren of the Detroit …
item.description: There are several restricted free agents the Lakers have already done their due diligence on, sources familiar with the team's thinking told ESPN. Two of them, Jalen Duren of the Detroit Pistons and Walker Kessler of the Utah Jazz, would satisfy Doncic's directive to secure an A-list center. Two others, Peyton Watson of the Denver Nuggets and Tari Eason of the Rockets, are the type of 3-and-D archetypes who any team needs in order to succeed in the modern NBA.

item.title: He was cleared for basketball activities on May 28 -- …
item.description: Luka Doncic is spending the summer in Slovenia with his daughters, Gabriela and Olivia, but sources close to him told ESPN he is in "constant communication" with Lakers president of basketball operations and general manager Rob Pelinka and coach JJ Redick. Coming off a season in which he led the league in scoring at 33.5 points per game and finished fourth in MVP voting before a Grade 2 left hamstring strain sidelined him for L.A.'s postseason run, Doncic is finally healthy as he begins his offs

item.title: A source close to Luka Doncic told ESPN that Doncic …
item.description: A source close to Luka Doncic told ESPN that Doncic has kept in touch with James and Reaves since the season ended but hasn't pitched either player on returning to L.A. Most of their conversations, the source said, revolve around golf, as Doncic took up the sport in February and has bonded with his fellow links-obsessed teammates over it.

item.title: "If Austin gets paid... then there's not enough money …
item.description: Run It Back: On a 1-10 scale, Sam Amick thinks the Lakers' chances for bringing back LeBron & Reaves are a 6 👀 "If Austin gets paid... then there's not enough money for LeBron to feel respected, and then I could see him going up the coast to the Warriors."

item.title: "Every conversation I have had with the Lakers, I felt …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: "Every conversation I have had with the Lakers, I felt like the option of using the cap space for a trade is very much in play" - @ramonashelburne

item.title: Hopefully Bron don't retire. Playing against Bron will …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: AJ Dybantsa: Hopefully Bron don't retire. Playing against Bron will be great. I don't think none of us get starstruck...but at the end of the day bro like it's Lebron. The fact that you're on the same court with Lebron is great"

item.title: Isaiah Stewart on the trade block
item.description: League sources say Detroit Pistons big man Isaiah Stewart is available. The Pistons, in search of additional shooting and playmakers, appear ready to rely on seventh-year big man Paul Reed in an increased role. Stewart has two years and a combined $30 million left on his deal (team option in the second season). Detroit, which is believed to be among the suitors for Los Angeles Lakers guard Austin Reaves if he makes it to unrestricted free agency, is also said to be interested in the Oklahoma Cit

item.title: San Antonio could use a backup stretch big as it …
item.description: San Antonio could use a backup stretch big as it reloads for next season, and a combo stretch big man who can start and possibly play alongside Wembanyama in bigger lineups such as Rui Hachimura. The opportunity to play alongside Wembanyama, the franchise's success, its culture and collective youth make San Antonio a preferred destination, sources said, adding that multiple big men are already trying to get to south Texas.

item.title: Paul Pierce says whatever the Lakers do this …
item.description: NBA Courtside: Paul Pierce says whatever the Lakers do this offseason, they have to get more athletic: “Because you’re looking at OKC and the Spurs, their perimeters, their centers. Like, you have to get more athletic and you have to get faster. Like, you can’t be an old team. You need somebody that can get up and down the court and pick up. Because it’s major guard play in the West. You got to focus on your guard play. Do you see what’s running the league right now? Brunson just won MVP. You go

item.title: Malik Beasley: I wish I had played better with the Lakers
item.description: Malik Beasley: I wish I had played better with the Lakers. I was going through a lot of mental sh*t though at that time. Neon: It's like, what is the hardest part mentally while you're in the NBA? Beasley: People don't even think about that. All the outside noise, like your regular life outside of basketball. Like for me, I didn't know where to live. So, I lived in Beverly Hills, but it was like 40 minutes away from the arena and 40 minutes away from the practice facility. So, you had to pick wh

item.title: Luka Doncic addresses decision not to play for Slovenia this summer
item.description: Luka Doncic revealed that family remains his primary focus this summer, while also expressing confidence in Slovenia’s chances despite his absence and confirming that his injury issues are now behind him. “We already know that I won’t play for the national team. The main reason is my daughters. Everything else is pretty much the same. I’m doing individual workouts, training in the gym, and also playing other sports. This year golf has become part of the routine, so it’s a little different. Golf

item.title: Doncic admitted he did not wake up in the middle of …
item.description: The New York Knicks defeated the San Antonio Spurs 4-1 in the NBA Finals, claiming their first championship since 1973. Brunson was named Finals MVP after averaging 32.6 points, 4.2 rebounds, and 4.6 assists in the series. However, because of the time difference in Slovenia, Luka Doncic admitted he did not wake up in the middle of the night to watch the games live. "It's all too early for me, waking up at three in the morning, so I didn't watch," Doncic said with a smile, via Siol.net.

item.title: He had already elbowed me in my sternum and knocked …
item.description: NBA Courtside: Matt Barnes on when he fake threw the ball in Kobe Bryant face in the finals: “The Orlando sh*t I told him in our last interview like bro I was ready to fu*king like I was ready to go, like fu*k basketball. Was ready to throw that ball in your face. Because he gets like he’s already so good but then he’ll elbow you and grab you and I remember I caught a dunk tip off the rim because he was guarding me and he tried to block Dwight shot and it fell of perfect and I put that b***h dow

item.title: Ramona Shelburne: "For me, it's pretty simple. It's …
item.description: "Do you have a sense for who those teams are, who are the real contenders if LeBron doesn't come back?" Ramona Shelburne: "For me, it's pretty simple. It's either the Lakers, Cleveland, or Golden State. I would be really surprised if it's not one of those three. I think there's other teams that would make sense, but I don't see those as realistic."

item.title: Luka Doncic on Austin Reaves: "I see him very well. He …
item.description: Luka Doncic on Austin Reaves: "I see him very well. He is a great player and he is a very good guy, so I think that it will go well for him."

item.title: Luka Doncic: 'I need shooters and some centers that jump a lot'
item.description: Luka Doncic: "I always need shooters around me because normally they double me quite a lot, so I think that I need shooters and some centers that jump a lot, defend, and put up blocks."

item.title: "They say that in the All-Star there could be one, so …
item.description: Luka Doncic on the possibility of participating in a 1-on-1 tournament: "They say that in the All-Star there could be one, so if they put it, it would be great."

item.title: Lakers among teams monitoring Lu Dort’s availability
item.description: With Williams expected to return healthy next season, Mitchell, Cason Wallace, Jared McCain, and Alex Caruso in the fold, teams across the league are monitoring fellow starter Lu Dort’s availability. Dort has a team option for $17.22 million for the upcoming season that several front executives who spoke with HoopsHype are projecting the Thunder to exercise, then consider flipping Dort on the trade market. Should such a scenario happen, Dort has been linked with the Lakers and several other team

item.title: Lakers impressed by Braden Smith
item.description: The Lakers spent time in Spain looking at 20-year-old guard Sergio de Larrea, but many NBA scouts see him going later in the first round or even in the second. According to people not authorized to speak publicly, the Lakers were impressed by their workout with Purdue point guard Braden Smith. But he’s on the smaller side (6 feet) and played four years in college, leading scouts to believe his upside is not that high and that he’ll be drafted in the second round.

item.title: Lakers in the mix for Mitchell Robinson, Robert Williams?
item.description: The Lakers have been linked to all sorts of veteran big men in the forthcoming free agent market as well. They could emerge as a suitor for the Knicks' Mitchell Robinson and the Trail Blazers' Robert Williams III, although sources say both players are very open to remaining in their incumbent roles with the Knicks and Blazers. I'm also told that the Lakers have called the Thunder about swingman Lu Dort's availability.

item.title: Peterson didn’t hesitate in calling James the GOAT
item.description: Peterson recently spoke about his admiration of James, saying that while he hasn’t yet met him, James commenting on his Instagram posts meant so much and that he hopes to have the career longevity of the all-time great, via NBA Future Now on X: Peterson didn’t hesitate in calling James the GOAT while also adding that his favorite team is the Lakers simply because that is who James plays for. That is the case for many fans these days who sometimes have more loyalty towards individual players than

item.title: Nothing has changed, as I said, I will not play this …
item.description: It was previously known that Luka Doncic would not be part of the Slovenian national team in early July, nor would he help Slovenia in the second part of the 2027 World Cup qualifiers in Qatar at the end of August. " Nothing has changed, as I said, I will not play this summer, " he added. Due to a hamstring injury, the Lakers' first star missed the entire North American league playoffs.

item.title: I won't hide it, if you don't win the title, it's a …
item.description: " I won't hide it, if you don't win the title, it's a bad season. We've been unlucky with injuries, myself included, after we played some great basketball in March. It's going to be a long time before next season. I hope it's better for us ." His club teammate, 2.13 m tall American center Jaxson Hayes , recently received Slovenian citizenship. " I wasn't involved in these talks at all. He's a good guy and that's what he is as a player. Otherwise, we have a young team that gives us hope. I want t

item.title: Even when I'm here, I hardly go anywhere, that's the …
item.description: He has a star status in Los Angeles and around the world, which also has a great impact on his private life, which he did not want to talk about in detail, including the breakup with his fiancée Anamarija Goltes . " Even when I'm here, I hardly go anywhere, that's the price for the position I'm in and what I wanted. It's completely different than in Los Angeles, where I feel good ."

item.title: Of course, I enjoy playing golf. There's nothing …
item.description: But he couldn't hide the excitement he found in the game of golf. " Of course, I enjoy playing golf. There's nothing better than spending four hours in nature. But I also choose to play 36 holes, which isn't exactly the best, and I play and enjoy it for eight hours. That's the most beautiful thing ," he said on the platform with the basketball court at the Stožice hall.

item.title: Lakers looking for an upgrade at the center position
item.description: That’s not to say neither Marcus Smart or Deandre Ayton will be back (both have player options), but team sources have repeatedly talked about an upgrade at the center position. They’ve also spoken about a desire to get younger and more athletic. If I had to pick one to be back, I’d lean Smart. I just think the Lakers want a more traditional Luka Dončić-style center.

item.title: Blazers interested in re-signing Robert Williams
item.description: Robert Williams III is an option, though Portland, according to league sources, is expected to show interest in re-signing him after he played 59 games last season — second-most in his injury-hampered career. It’s a bit of bad luck for the Lakers to be looking for a rim-running, rim-protecting center at a time when there aren’t many of them in the league — not to mention ones that are available.

item.title: Lakers were “on the clock” with Dončić right after the …
item.description: Lakers were “on the clock” with Dončić right after the end of last season, and nothing I’ve heard from my sources would change that thought process. Dončić is in the prime of his career and was traded away from a roster built to his specifications to compete for NBA titles. And if the Lakers can’t provide that, maybe he looks elsewhere (if anybody should understand that this is business, it’s Dončić).

item.title: Dwight Howard: We need to do a 2020 lakers vacation …
item.description: Dwight Howard: We need to do a 2020 lakers vacation since we didn’t get a parade 👍🏾

item.title: Malique Lewis is completing a late flurry of workouts …
item.description: Malique Lewis is completing a late flurry of workouts with NBA teams, with his stock seemingly gathering real momentum just days out from the 2026 NBA Draft. The Trinidadian wing -- who's coming off two seasons with the South East Melbourne Phoenix as part of the NBL's Next Stars program -- has a workout with the Los Angeles Lakers on Tuesday (AEDT), sources told ESPN. It'll be the sixth and final he completes following a standout showing at EuroCamp in Treviso.

item.title: Thunder trade Aaron Wiggins to Atlanta
item.description: Shams Charania: The Oklahoma City Thunder are finalizing a trade to send guard Aaron Wiggins to the Atlanta Hawks for two second-round picks (Atlanta's in 2030 and the least favorable of Hawks/Lakers in 2032), sources tell ESPN.

item.title: The Lakers have hired former New Orleans Pelicans …
item.description: Benjamin Royer: The Lakers have hired former New Orleans Pelicans basketball analytics coordinator Dominic Samangy as a basketball analyst.

item.title: "I haven't thought about it yet. I think for now I …
item.description: Doncic was also asked about his plans and will he consider joining Zvezda at some point during his career: "I haven't thought about it yet. I think for now I still have some career left in the NBA, I'm not that old yet. But maybe one day I will come," he concluded.

item.title: "A lot. I think he arrived during my second season. …
item.description: Doncic notably highlighted Nikola Jokic and how he enjoys playing with him: "When I watch Nikola Jokic play and when I play against him I feel great. We joke a bit with each other but the way he's playing is phenomenal. He's playing incredible and does stuff that he sees before anyone else does." Jokic also used a chance to thank Boban Marjanovic for helping him accommodate himself in the US: "A lot. I think he arrived during my second season. Bobi is a great guy, we all know that. We were alway

item.title: "I still stand by Spanoulis. He was a really great …
item.description: Who was your idol growing up? We heard you said it was Vasilis Spanoulis , but was there anyone else whose game you followed and whose moves you liked to copy? "I still stand by Spanoulis. He was a really great player and I loved watching him."

item.title: Marc Stein has dismissed the Lakers' inclusion in the …
item.description: Contrary to recent reports and speculation, the Los Angeles Lakers appear not to be in any potential trade frameworks that would help complete the Giannis Antetokounmpo blockbuster trade. Appearing on ALL NBA Podcast, seasoned NBA insider Marc Stein has dismissed the Lakers' inclusion in the ongoing Giannis trade sweepstakes. "I have to be honest based on what I’ve heard. I haven’t heard that," Stein revealed about LA potentially being involved. "I know there was all kinds of speculation yesterd

item.title: LeBron James has not fully committed to returning to the Lakers next season
item.description: NBA Base: LeBron James has not fully committed to returning to the Lakers next season, per @ramonashelburne “My understanding, had initial conversations with LeBron James' representative, but he has not even fully committed to returning next year in these conversations. The conversations were described to me as 'we're keeping in touch.' Okay, so there's no commitment to even returning or salary figures being discussed.”

item.title: Sam Amick and I reported that the Lakers were “on the …
item.description: Sam Amick and I reported that the Lakers were “on the clock” with Dončić right after the end of last season, and nothing I’ve heard from my sources would change that thought process. Dončić is in the prime of his career and was traded away from a roster built to his specifications to compete for NBA titles. And if the Lakers can’t provide that, maybe he looks elsewhere (if anybody should understand that this is business, it’s Dončić).

item.title: “I seen the effect that it had on LeBron when Kyrie …
item.description: Boston Celtics All-Star Jaylen Brown has been the subject of rumors since his season ended, which could split as one half of the 2024 champion All-NBA tandem alongside Jayson Tatum, of which Rich Paul is warning the front office. While looking back on his client LeBron James' final season with the Cleveland Cavs, Paul, James' agent, reflected on how the loss of Kyrie Irving humbled LeBron. For Paul, it was a split between two All-Stars that could have been avoided if the two had spoken to one an

item.title: Hawks are the most active team in discussing trade …
item.description: League sources say the Hawks are the most active team in discussing trade scenarios. They completed a deal with the Oklahoma City Thunder on Sunday night to acquire Aaron Wiggins into the trade exception they generated when they dealt Luke Kennard to the Los Angeles Lakers at the deadline. Sources have said Atlanta is discussing both trade-up and trade-down scenarios. But it also could just stay here and take the player it wants if he remains on the board.

item.title: LeBron James says he doesn’t know when the most …
item.description: NBA Courtside: LeBron James says he doesn’t know when the most important person in the summertime was the trainer and not the player: “Like the basketball trainer is the most important guy in the summer. I don’t know when that dynamic shifted because you know we used to play and a lot of my friends can attest to this like even when I got in the NBA you know, I built a little basketball court in my backyard in Akron at my house. I would get nine other guys and we would play in the summertime on a

item.title: James on the narrative around Cam Boozer size and …
item.description: NBA Courtside: LeBron James on the narrative around Cam Boozer size and athleticism: “I played with his dad it was kind of the same. They have the same projection. Wasn’t the tallest, you know, under size four. Wasn’t the most athletic, you know, and then we see what he was able to do, you know, I think he’s multiple All-Star, got the max a couple times. Utah gave him the max, you know, so like great career. The narrative of what you or what people are saying you are is never the end of the stor

item.title: The Heat could try to lure impending free agent LeBron …
item.description: The Heat could try to lure impending free agent LeBron James with its full midlevel exception, but a James return is considered a long shot. The Heat also has available a $5.5 million biannual exception as well as a $5.6 million exception from the Haywood Highsmith trade with Brooklyn. That exception expires Aug. 17.

item.title: “LeBron will stay with the Lakers, I think. It’s hard …
item.description: Which team do you think LeBron will be with next season? NBA executive: “LeBron will stay with the Lakers, I think. It’s hard to imagine him leaving. If he wants his money, it’s going to be hard for him to leave. I have to imagine there’s some kind of compromise they can do on, compensation-wise. It’s just easier. I don’t know where else he would be going. I think he would rather play with his son and not have to move.”

item.title: “I think he would be good in Golden State. He’s smart …
item.description: NBA assistant coach: “I think he would be good in Golden State. He’s smart enough to be able to play with those other smart minds and maximize everything. He could use his brain with those guys and not have to use as much athleticism. It’s a perfect spot for him. I don’t know if he can put up numbers. But as far as enjoying the game, using his brain and being effective, it’s a good fit. He can make plays happen instead of reacting to plays. He’d have everything that he needs. He’s a brilliant pa

item.title: “I think LeBron stays with the Lakers. I don’t think …
item.description: NBA scout: “I think LeBron stays with the Lakers. I don’t think he’d leave his son. I could be wrong. But I don’t think LeBron is giving anybody a discount. He wants his respect and wants to do it on his terms. The Lakers may wish they could get off of him, but he still has control over that deal. The Lakers are the best option. But if all three are healthy with Luka, Austin [Reaves] and LeBron, I don’t think the Lakers are a title contender. Luka is amazing. But I don’t see him enough for the L

item.title: Myles Turner and Kyle Kuzma trade candidates for Bucks?
item.description: With Giannis Antetokounmpo and Bobby Portis gone, all eyes turn to holdovers Myles Turner and Kyle Kuzma as trade candidates for the Bucks. Turner has always known that if Antetokounmpo was ever traded, he’d be a strong trade candidate, HoopsHype has learned. During his Pacers tenure, Turner was linked to the Hornets, Pelicans, and Lakers in trade rumors at various points. Those teams could all make sense now, and possibly the Celtics. Turner, 30, is still owed $83.55 million, including a player

item.title: Elsewhere, within league circles, there was palpable …
item.description: Elsewhere, within league circles, there was palpable chatter about the Pistons hoping to get involved in the Antetokounmpo trade to land Tyler Herro, while dangling assets such as Isaiah Stewart, Ron Holland, Duncan Robinson, and draft compensation, league sources told HoopsHype. In addition, the Los Angeles Lakers were linked with trade interest in Kel’el Ware over the past couple of days, sources said.

item.title: Warriors have discussed No. 11 pick to Pelicans for Trey Murphy
item.description: Golden State has also been one of many interested suitors in New Orleans Pelicans forward Trey Murphy III, and the two teams have discussed concepts involving the No. 11 pick, league sources told HoopsHype. As previously reported by HoopsHype, the Pelicans have searched for a trade into the lottery and are targeting a player. Nate Ament has been mentioned as one potential candidate for the Pelicans. Before this past trade deadline, several teams, including the Warriors, Pistons, Pacers, Hawks, T

item.title: Bucks plan to keep Kel'el Ware for now
item.description: Jake Fischer: I do believe Milwaukee has tried to package Nos. 10 and 13 to move up in tonight's first round, but I don't believe there's going to be any trades in the top-10. Sources say the Bucks, at this point, plan to keep Kel'el Ware, despite interest from rival teams like the Lakers. Milwaukee coveted Ware and Kasparas Jakučionis in their recent drafts. The Bucks also value Tyler Herro, sources say, and don't necessarily plan to move him, while Milwaukee will field offers for the All-Star

item.title: LeBron James: "Guys are not taking much time off after …
item.description: LeBron James: "Guys are not taking much time off after the season now to get back on the court because it's too much build up and the game is so much faster now. You can't be, you know, you used to have the ability to kind of use training camp and use like preseason to kind of get into the rhythm, you know, but now with so many, we're playing so much faster, you know, and soft tissue injuries are happening to reoccurring now that you have to start the the the body movement a lot sooner than than

item.title: LeBron James: "I don't know when that changed, that it …
item.description: LeBron James: "I don't know when that changed, that it was more beneficial to do the individual workouts over the five-on-five play through the summer. I don't know when the most important person in the summertime was the trainer and not the player. I know y'all are going to be like, 'Bron, we ain't seen no five-on-five clips of you lately.' Yeah, and you ain't."

item.title: LeBron James to Steve Nash: "The 96 draft, besides you …
item.description: LeBron James to Steve Nash: "The 96 draft, besides you guys being one of the greatest drafts of all time, is that Slam cover shoot that you guys did. I had that in my room when I stayed in Spring Hills, it was on my wall with all you guys there."

item.title: Myles Turner thought he would be traded to the Celtics and Lakers at different points in his career
item.description: Myles Turner on dealing with constant NBA trade rumors: "I'm so numb to it now. At the very beginning of my career, I was in a trade rumor every single offseason. I had my bags packed twice. I thought at one point in my career I was going to be traded to Boston for Gordon Hayward and I thought I literally was ready to go and then that fell through at the last second. And then I also thought I was going to be traded to the Lakers for a while. So I'm so numb to it now, if it happens it happens and

item.title: Lakers among teams that reached out about Yves Missi
item.description: Multiple teams, including the Lakers, have reached out to the Pelicans about Yves Missi, but the Pelicans have resisted those offers. Also worth noting on New Orleans: the club had been exploring its options to move up in the first round of the draft. Some members of the organization are fans of Nate Ament.

item.title: New York is trading No. 24 to the Los Angeles Lakers …
item.description: Shams Charania: Sources: New York is trading No. 24 to the Los Angeles Lakers for No. 25.

item.title: Lakers select Cameron Carr at No. 24 after trade with Knicks
item.description: Benjamin Royer: Lakers select Baylor guard Cameron Carr with the 24th pick in the NBA draft, after trading with the New York Knicks moments ago.

item.title: Knicks received cash considerations from the Lakers …
item.description: Shams Charania: The Knicks received cash considerations from the Lakers for No. 24 -- Cameron Carr.

item.title: Knicks draft Sergio De Larrea at No. 25
item.description: Michael Scotto: Sources: The Los Angeles Lakers will select Sergio De Larrea with the No. 25 pick in the 2026 NBA Draft. He and cash considerations are heading to the New York Knicks as part of the trade.

item.title: Cameron Carr on whom he’d rather guard, SGA or Anthony …
item.description: Lakers draft pick Cameron Carr on whom he’d rather guard, SGA or Anthony Edwards: "Yeah, I ain't dealing with the flopping, Anthony Edwards"

item.title: “Lakers Collective” is one of the biggest Los …
item.description: There are quite a few podcasts dedicated to covering the Los Angeles Lakers, which makes sense since they are one of the most popular franchises in professional sports. “Lakers Collective” is one of the biggest Los Angeles-themed shows, but it is unfortunately losing one of its hosts, Jason Timpf. Sadly, his departure is due to some serious health issues that he announced on Tuesday.

item.title: Timpf revealed that he has been battling a virus and …
item.description: Timpf revealed that he has been battling a virus and myocarditis, and he was bedridden for six days around the NBA Finals. “I got just an absolutely gnarly viral illness. Like, the worst viral illness I’ve ever had in my life. It was absolutely brutal,” Timpf said. “During that process, I ended up getting a condition called myocarditis, which is essentially inflammation of the heart muscle. It’s caused, like, some palpitations and other crap that I have been dealing with. “It’s made it so I can’

item.title: Austin Reaves intends to re-sign with Lakers on four-year max extension
item.description: Shams Charania: Los Angeles Lakers star Austin Reaves intends to sign a four-year, $185 million maximum contract to return to the franchise, with a player option for the final season in 2029-30, sources tell ESPN. Reaves declined his $14.9M player option for the new max deal.

item.title: Obviously, LeBron would have to want to sign with the …
item.description: Brian Windhorst: Obviously, LeBron would have to want to sign with the Cavs, but if your pathway to paying LeBron the money is to trade Jarrett Allen for him, the Lakers would KILL for Jarrett Allen. KILL for him. All right? They would do that deal in 17-tenths of a second.

item.title: Austin Reaves intends to sign four-year max contract to return to Lakers
item.description: Shams Charania: Los Angeles Lakers star Austin Reaves intends to sign a four-year, $185 million maximum contract to return to the franchise, with a player option for the final season in 2029-30, sources tell ESPN. Reaves declined his $14.9M player option for the new max deal.

item.title: The Lakers and Reaves' agents, Aaron Reilly and Reggie …
item.description: Shams Charania: The Lakers and Reaves' agents, Aaron Reilly and Reggie Berry of AMR Agency, worked through discussions over the last 10 days after teams were able to negotiate with their own free agents. Reaves lands the richest contract for an undrafted player in NBA history.

item.title: Detroit continues to express trade interest in Tyler Herro
item.description: Marc Stein: With Reaves now sure to stay in LA, as was long believed to be his clear preference, league sources say Detroit continues to express trade interest in Milwaukee's soon-to-be acquired Tyler Herro. The Bucks are expected to listen to offers but could also elect to keep Herro.

item.title: Reaves was expected to receive free-agent interest …
item.description: Shams Charania: Reaves was expected to receive free-agent interest from multiple teams -- such as Detroit -- on maximum-level salary, so the Lakers stepped up on Wednesday to lock in their homegrown talent. Reaves went from undrafted to a two-way player -- to making the Lakers roster and now multiple lucrative deals in L.A.

item.title: The Lakers and Austin Reaves agreed to a four-year …
item.description: Keith Smith: The Lakers and Austin Reaves agreed to a four-year maximum contract his new $185M contract. Here's how that deal will look: 26-27: $41.3M 27-28: $44.6M 28-29: $47.9M 29-30: $51.2M (player option) Reaves' deal will get signed after the Lakers use their cap space, should they choose that route. LA still has lots of flexibility for building the roster this summer.

item.title: I’m told that the many moving parts that landed Cam …
item.description: Marc Stein: I’m told that the many moving parts that landed Cam Carr with the Lakers at No. 24, Sergio De Larrea with the Mavericks at No. 25, Koa Peat with the Suns at No. 30 and netted the Knicks five second-round picks is tracking to take the form of a four-team TRADE once made official.

item.title: Austin Reaves felt “great relief” when his reps and …
item.description: Dave McMenamin: Austin Reaves felt “great relief” when his reps and the Lakers agreed to a four-year, $185 million extension, a source close to Reaves told ESPN. “He wants to be a Laker for life and there's nothing more important to him than trying to win one for that city,” the source said.

item.title: Bulls trading No. 56 pick to Lakers for cash
item.description: Shams Charania: Sources: The Los Angeles Lakers are acquiring No. 56 from the Chicago Bulls in the second round of the NBA draft tonight at 8 pm ET on ESPN. Lakers sent cash to the Bulls to get a pick tonight.

item.title: Cash being sent is from Lakers is from their cash …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: The cash being sent is from Lakers is from their cash limit from the current 2025-26 salary cap year. They have $4.7 million remaining to distribute to the Knicks in yesterday's trade and in this one. Cash limit to be sent/received will reset to $8.5 million on July 1.

item.title: “And it kind of meets the common goal of why they gave …
item.description: “It’s going to be fun. And like I said, winning a championship is the main goal,” Reaves said. “And I feel like, you know, with me and Luka, that’s a great starting piece to compete. And when we put a roster together that can go compete, I feel like winning a championship will basically solidify everything that I want and everything that the Lakers want. “And it kind of meets the common goal of why they gave me the money they did.”

item.title: “I mean, it was a breath of fresh air because I hadn’t …
item.description: “I mean, it was a breath of fresh air because I hadn’t stopped thinking about where I was going to be. And it’s just like a sigh of relief,” he said in a phone interview Wednesday. “I mean, regardless of whatever played out, I’m obviously grateful and in a good position to take care of the people I love. My heart’s been in L.A. Every morning I wake up, I’m just like, ‘I hope we can get this done today.’ “And even if I was frustrated through the process when we (weren’t) getting the numbers we wa

item.title: I'm being told the Lakers are working to trade up even …
item.description: Jake Fischer: We're expecting a ton of trade activity throughout tonight's second round. Among that movement: I'm being told the Lakers are working to trade up even higher in the second round, after already acquiring the No. 56 pick tonight.

item.title: He said Detroit was going to make a run at Austin …
item.description: Alex Kennedy: The Pistons will try to go big-game hunting after the Isaiah Stewart trade, per @WindhorstESPN on ESPN's draft broadcast. He said Detroit was going to make a run at Austin Reaves before the Lakers locked him up. He mentioned Kawhi Leonard and LeBron James as possible targets.

item.title: Had been a lot of noise with the Lakers and Veesar, …
item.description: Dan Woike: Had been a lot of noise with the Lakers and Veesar, but the Hawks jump them and get him in a trade with the Clippers.

item.title: Atlanta traded No. 57 and cash to the Clippers for No. …
item.description: Jake Fischer: Atlanta traded No. 57 and cash to the Clippers for No. 52, leapfrogging the Lakers at No. 56, who a lot of teams thought were targeting Henri Veesaar as well.

item.title: Mavs selects Vsevolod Ishchenko at No. 56 via trade with Lakers
item.description: Shams Charania: The Lakers and Mavericks have made a trade -- L.A. sends No. 56 Vsevolod Ishchenko to Dallas.

item.title: Los Angeles Lakers will select Vsevolod Ishchenko with …
item.description: Michael Scotto: The Los Angeles Lakers will select Vsevolod Ishchenko with the 56th pick in the 2026 NBA Draft.

item.title: Mavericks sent cash to the Lakers for Vsevolod …
item.description: Shams Charania: The Mavericks sent cash to the Lakers for Vsevolod Ishchenko at 56, sources said.

item.title: Dallas Mavericks are trading for Vsevolod Ishchenko, …
item.description: Michael Scotto: The Dallas Mavericks are trading for Vsevolod Ishchenko, who was just selected by the Los Angeles Lakers with the 56th pick.

item.title: AK Okereke agrees to two-way deal to join Lakers
item.description: Shams Charania: Undrafted Vanderbilt forward AK Okereke has agreed to a two-way NBA deal with the Los Angeles Lakers, sources tell ESPN.

item.title: The Los Angeles Lakers have agreed to a two-way …
item.description: Michael Scotto‬: Sources: The Los Angeles Lakers have agreed to a two-way contract with MAC Player of the Year Peter Suder. He led Miami (OH) to a 31-0 regular-season record and shot 42% from 3-point range this season. Keith Kreiter and Sam Cipriano of Edge Sports completed the deal.

item.title: Austin Reaves collapsed on the grass at The Course at …
item.description: Austin Reaves collapsed on the grass at The Course at Eagle Mountain. Talks with the Los Angeles Lakers about his free agency lasted only a week, but Reaves was fried. Tuesday needed to be the last of it — for his sanity. “I didn’t like it,” Reaves told The Athletic. “It was weird. It just was, I mean, it’s just a lot of ‘What ifs.’ I just like to know where I’m going to be.”

item.title: LeBron would have to want to sign with the Cavs. But …
item.description: Another wrench was thrown into this story June 24, when ESPN NBA insider Brian Windhorst made a bombshell claim during a segment on an ESPN Cleveland radio show. “The thought process in Los Angeles — and I think if the Cavs were interested in this, the Lakers would sit up in attention right now — that they would sign and trade LeBron for Jarrett Allen,” Windhorst said, per an X post from ESPN Cleveland. “And I think if the Cavs were willing to do that, they could have LeBron. Obviously, LeBron w

item.title: Speaking to La Gazzetta dello Sport during a visit to …
item.description: The newly revealed team name, Basketball Club Roma SPQR, marks a groundbreaking moment for Italian basketball by successfully uniting global sports ambition with a deep reverence for continental traditions. Spearheaded by an ownership group featuring Donnie Nelson and Slovenian superstar Luka Doncic, the new team aims to elevate Rome back to the forefront of the game. Speaking to La Gazzetta dello Sport during a visit to the capital, the leader of the Los Angeles Lakers in the NBA shared his dee

item.title: Lakers signing William Kyle
item.description: Dave McMenamin: Former Syracuse center William Kyle III is signing with the Lakers on an Exhibit 10 deal, sources told ESPN. The 6-9, 230-pound big man averaged 8.4 points on 66.1% with 7.1 rebounds and 2.5 blocks per game in his senior season with the Orange

item.title: The #Mavs have officially acquired the draft rights to …
item.description: Mike Curtis: The #Mavs have officially acquired the draft rights to 56th overall pick Vsevolod Ishchenko in a trade with the #Lakers in exchange for cash considerations. Ishchenko, a 6-foot-8 wing, averaged 8.7 points and 4.7 rebounds on 46.3% from 3 for Russia’s Lokomotiv Kuban.

item.title: "I knew a few off the top of my head, but I hadn't …
item.description: He will become the 13th player in NBA history to reach a 20th season, joining LeBron James, Vince Carter, Chris Paul, Dirk Nowitzki, Kevin Garnett, Kevin Willis, Robert Parish, Kyle Lowry, Udonis Haslem, Jamal Crawford, Kobe Bryant and Kareem Abdul-Jabbar. "I knew a few off the top of my head, but I hadn't seen the list," Al Horford told ESPN. "It's hard to believe. It's something that, for me, I'm very grateful. It's just so hard to be in this league for that amount of years. There's a lot of c

item.title: The Lakers held a meeting with Austin Reaves playing …
item.description: NBA Courtside: The Lakers held a meeting with Austin Reaves playing his favorite country songs, and made him Pillows with AR initials on them in their recruiting pitch, per @ShamsCharania

item.title: Robert McCray V has agreed to an Exhibit-10 contract …
item.description: Jon Chepkevich: NEWS: Florida State’s Robert McCray V has agreed to an Exhibit-10 contract with the Los Angeles Lakers, sources tell DraftExpress. The 6’4” guard led the Seminoles in scoring and assists en route to All-ACC honors. Super bouncy athlete with a 43” max vert.

item.title: Jacari White has signed a Summer League deal with the …
item.description: Dushawn London: Virginia guard Jacari White has signed a Summer League deal with the Los Angeles Lakers, source tells @247Sports

item.title: Kareem Abdul-Jabbar puts four of his NBA championship rings up for auction
item.description: Hall of Fame center Kareem Abdul-Jabbar has put four of his NBA championship rings up for auction. Abdul-Jabbar has hundreds of items from his memorabilia collection available in the auction, being conducted by Goldin Auctions, with "much of the proceeds" going to his Skyhook Foundation charity to help benefit kids learn about science, technology, engineering and math. Besides the 1980, 1985, 1987 and 1988 championship rings he earned as a member of the Los Angeles Lakers, Abdul-Jabbar is auctio

item.title: "Looking back on what I have done with my life, …
item.description: When it comes to choosing between storing a championship ring or trophy in a room, or providing kids with an opportunity to change their lives, the choice is pretty simple. Sell it all," Abdul-Jabbar wrote on his blog about why he's auctioning off valuables from his sports history. "Looking back on what I have done with my life, instead of gazing at the sparkle of jewels or gold plating celebrating something I did a long time ago, I'd rather look into the delighted face of a child holding their

item.title: "We do this by sending children from economically …
item.description: Some of the championship rings start with a minimum bid of $60,000. Also available on the website are game-worn jerseys, uniforms, trophies and even various keys to cities that he received. "Since my life is still happening and ever-evolving, I am less personally attached to those items than I am to my desire to create new history for myself -- and futures for others," Abdul-Jabbar said. "Much of the proceeds from my auction will go to support my charity, the Skyhook Foundation, whose mission is

item.title: Dan Gilbert on reuniting with LeBron James: 'If you're both mutually on the same mission, it can work out'
item.description: Interviewer: How did two such strong personalities as Dan Gilbert—owner of the Cleveland Cavs, mega-billionaire, business owner, operator—and LeBron James have that kind of falling out and then make it work again? Dan Gilbert: Well, I flew to Miami, where he was with two or three of his advisers, and we had a couple-hour meeting. I think, at the end of the day, it was in both of our interests to do this because he wanted to come back. He wanted to come back home, and we’re the only place he coul

item.title: Dan Gilbert admits his infamous letter after LeBron James' departure was 'a stupid thing'
item.description: Did you prep for going into that meeting? Did you have a sense of what you were going to say, or did you just play it? Dan Gilbert: I’m sure I prepped it a little bit. Interviewer: Did you apologize early on, or before the meeting? Dan Gilbert: About the letter? Interviewer: Yeah. Dan Gilbert: Yeah. I mean, I admitted that the letter was a stupid thing. And for me personally, at that time, if you Googled my name—I mean, I think I’ve done some other stuff in my career—and that would be the only t

item.title: Dan Gilbert: Yeah. And it was stupid overall. If I had …
item.description: Interviewer: You wrote a very strongly worded letter. Dan Gilbert: Yes. Interviewer: I’m not going to make you read it. Dan Gilbert: No. Interviewer: But it talked about betrayal. Dan Gilbert: Well, what happened was, remember, he went on national television and did a show about where he was going to go play next. Interviewer: Yeah. Is that where you found out, though? Dan Gilbert: Yes. Interviewer: You found out when he announced it? Dan Gilbert: Yes. They never told us in advance. Interviewer:

item.title: So two people read it. Were you counseled not to put …
item.description: Interviewer: You gave him some heat. What were you thinking? Was that you doing it? Did some intern draft that letter? Dan Gilbert: I actually—you should have seen the first version. No, I’m kidding. Interviewer: Wait, did you have other people read this letter? Dan Gilbert: Yeah, I had one or two people, but they didn’t tell me what they should have told me. Interviewer: Who were the one or two people? Your brother? Dan Gilbert: No, one of the PR people. And I think maybe my ex-wife read it. In

item.title: Dan Gilbert reveals Comic Sans in his LeBron James letter was a joke by a team member weeks earlier
item.description: Well, the whole conversation around it was also around the font choice of the letter. Dan Gilbert: That was another thing. Interviewer: Comic Sans. Dan Gilbert: What was weird is that somebody on my team, just as a joke, I think the week or two before, changed my font to Comic Sans. Interviewer: What was your original font going to be? Dan Gilbert: Probably Times New Roman or something. So it wasn’t changed just for that letter. It was changed weeks ahead of that. Interviewer: So everything was

item.title: How’s your relationship now with LeBron? You guys …
item.description: How’s your relationship now with LeBron? You guys still— Dan Gilbert: I think it’s fine. We don’t see each other that much. He works in California. I work in Detroit and Cleveland. Interviewer: Yeah. Dan Gilbert: He came to a game or two last year. Interviewer: Was it hard for you to watch? In the game before the Eastern Conference Finals, it was— Dan Gilbert: Game seven. Interviewer: It was game seven, and it was the Cleveland Cavaliers— Dan Gilbert: And the Detroit Pistons. Interviewer: And yo

item.title: "I’ve interviewed for so many jobs, I think it was a …
item.description: For Nori, this is the realization of a dream almost three decades in the making as a lifer assistant coach with five different organizations across 28 years in the NBA, most recently with the Minnesota Timberwolves for the last five seasons. He's interviewed for numerous head coaching jobs in that time, including the Lakers and Cavaliers openings in 2024 and the Knicks job that went to Mike Brown last summer, perpetually finishing second or third in those searches. "I’ve interviewed for so many

item.title: Lakers have not made LeBron James an offer yet and communication has been limited
item.description: The Lakers can create up to $50 million in space and James may be willing to be flexible on his contract depending on how the team uses the space to upgrade its roster as Luka Doncic begins his second full season with the team. "There was a check-in call made, to my understanding, early in free agency once teams were able to start negotiating with their own free agents after the end of the NBA Finals," said ESPN's Shams Charania on NBA Today. "Since then though there hasn't been much communicati

item.title: “Right now, the Cavs can only offer LeBron four …
item.description: Windhorst discussed James’ rumored reunion with the Cleveland Cavaliers on “The Pat McAfee Show,” and said he believes it’s highly unlikely to occur. “Right now, the Cavs can only offer LeBron four million bucks,” Windhorst said. “One of the things I’ve learned about LeBron is you never count his money and never spend his money. So, making assumptions that LeBron would take a lot less money, I see people making that assumption, and I just don’t think it’s smart.”

item.title: The only real way that I can see that the Cavs could …
item.description: Windhorst added, “He is still a max-level player. … The only real way that I can see that the Cavs could get LeBron the money would be to do a trade with Jarrett Allen. … But I don’t think that would improve the Cavs. … I don’t see the pathway.”

item.title: Lakers adding Robbie Avila
item.description: Jon Chepkevich: NEWS: Saint Louis’ Robbie Avila has agreed to an Exhibit-10 contract with the Los Angeles Lakers, sources tell DraftExpress. The A10 Player of the Year is one of the nation’s most skilled bigs. Shot 40% from three and averaged 4+ assists per game as a frontcourt playmaking hub.

item.title: Austin Reaves is already No. 1 in guaranteed career …
item.description: Jorge Sierra: Austin Reaves is already No. 1 in guaranteed career earnings among undrafted players.

item.title: Richard Jefferson on thhe group chat dynamics of the …
item.description: Richard Jefferson on thhe group chat dynamics of the 2016 Cavs: "When we talk about the group chat from the team, there's like six of us, and it's been the same six. There was the full team group chat, then there was the guys that were J.R., Swish, Tristan, Kevin, Channing, and Bron. So that's the group of us. And then we start doing, hey guys, we should get together, do some golf and go do this, and then a conversation kind of got started is, and like, hey, it would be wrong if we didn't invite

item.title: So my understanding is there hasn't been an offer out …
item.description: Shams Charania: So my understanding is there hasn't been an offer out to LeBron James yet as far as his free agency. We'll see how that develops over the next four or five days when free agency starts. But a lot of it is like how much can we upgrade the roster and then potentially go back to LeBron or do you take care of LeBron first and then figure out any type of potential roster move?

item.title: Lakers an option for Mitchell Robinson?
item.description: The Lakers, amid a growing pessimism that the Knicks will be able to hang onto Mitchell Robinson, are also regarded as a likely suitor for the New York big man once free agency officially begins.

item.title: Damjan Rudez: 'Nobody had a nice word to say about Kobe Bryant as a teammate'
item.description: Damjan Rudez: 'Kobe Bryant had a magnetic aura that is hard to describe; he possessed a gravitational force that made everyone in his vicinity react differently. For my generation, Kobe was our Michael Jordan. I watched every move he made, from his 81-point game to all his records, and dreamt of playing against him. During his playing days, he was impenetrable; he didn't allow the public into his private life or show a soft side. He was a monster—a serious psychopath and a killer on the court wh

item.title: “I can see him [LeBron James] coming back. It’ll …
item.description: The Lakers made a check-in call to LeBron James early in free agency and haven’t followed up with an offer since. That silence is opening a door Pat Riley may be ready to walk through. The 2x Miami Heat champion Mario Chalmers will never stop rooting for a LeBron reunion in South Florida. Now the Giannis Antetokounmpo trade has rallied him to suggest that LeBron James and his son, Bronny James, could make a shocking move to the Heat. Appearing on WQAM radio, Chalmers was asked what blockbuster a

item.title: “You could actually get Bronny some real minutes at PG …
item.description: Chalmers implied that Bronny would be part of this potential move. He went on to highlight how the infrastructure in Miami would serve as the perfect environment to nurture Bronny’s development. “You could actually get Bronny some real minutes at PG and keep developing him and let him learn from other guys, so I think it’s a great move.”

item.title: The Lakers are actively exploring trade scenarios …
item.description: Fullcourtpass: The Lakers are actively exploring trade scenarios utilizing their cap space to improve the roster, per @ShamsCharania

item.title: It might even be more likely than not, that he leaves …
item.description: Heat Central: “There is definitely a lot of belief around the leagues, around Los Angeles stemming out of Klutch Sports, that LeBron has so many outcomes on the table here. It might even be more likely than not, that he leaves Los Angeles than stays with the Lakers” — @JakeLFischer on LeBrons future

item.title: “Cam is used to being around winning programs, winning …
item.description: “Cam is used to being around winning programs, winning cultures, and he’s got winning DNA,” Scott Drew said. “He can score four points and be happy the team won. He can defend and rebound and pass and be happy. He can be on the bench and cheer on his teammates, so he cares about winning. And I know at the end of the day, a great organization like the Lakers, that’s what they care most about. It’s a great fit for him.”

item.title: Carr said he didn’t workout with the Lakers in the …
item.description: Dan Woike: Carr said he didn’t workout with the Lakers in the pre-draft process but had meetings with them in the build.

item.title: “Yeah, first things first, is play defense.
item.description: Benjamin Royer: Cameron Carr on what he needs to do to get on the floor right away from the Lakers: “Yeah, first things first, is play defense. I feel like I got a show that I'm the best defender here, and that's what I'm here to do.”

item.title: Carr said he's focused on adding strength in the …
item.description: Mark Medina: Cameron Carr said he's looking forward "to learn" from Luka Doncic, Austin Reaves and possibly LeBron James. Carr said he's focused on adding strength in the weight room.

item.title: And then once I finally fell in love with it and found …
item.description: Law Murray: Asked Lakers 1st round pick Cameron Carr about his father Chris Carr, who played for six seasons with six teams "First things first, I can accredit him, is he kind of let me fall in love with the game of basketball myself. And then once I finally fell in love with it and found my *why* on why I wanted to play basketball, then I feel like I could go to him with more conversation, connection, or questions. And it made our bond stronger. He was a good outlet for me because he's been whe

item.title: Lakers offered Austin Reaves $150 million for five years before rival teams entered the picture
item.description: Brian Windhorst: "I do think the Austin Reaves contract, what we saw, was a fascinating situation of leverage. The Lakers, from my knowledge, he signed a four years, 185. From what I was told, the Lakers were in the range of five years, 150 for Austin Reaves a couple of days ago. 30 million dollars a year. Very good contract, significantly higher than what he was making right now. And I'm, I mean, it wasn't their final offer, clearly, but that's where they were in the range of. And some teams in

item.title: "The most annoying part was Delly and Bron waking up …
item.description: Richard Jefferson: "The most annoying part was Delly and Bron waking up to work out every day. That was the most annoying part. It's like, really, you're going to go work out?"

item.title: Marcus Smart an option for Houston?
item.description: The Rockets do, however, loom as a possible free agent destination for another one of Udoka's former players in Boston: Marcus Smart. League sources say that Smart, who is expected to draw interest from Houston and possibly other teams in free agency, is very much giving consideration to declining his $5.4 million player option for next season with the Lakers before Monday's deadline to do so and then proceed to the open market.

item.title: "Trust me, those guys asked LeBron what he was …
item.description: ESPN Cleveland: "Trust me, those guys asked LeBron what he was planning on doing and LeBron did not tell them," - @WindhorstESPN on LeBron James' future plans.

item.title: Lowe floated the possibility of the Clippers signing …
