"I don't know if this has been decided yet, but someone who would know told me [Wednesday] that the Clippers are telling or behaving I guess as though they're going to have cap space in the offseason," said Zach Lowe. "They can't open much. They can open about $20 million but they have to cut some options and renounce some

item.title: The Nuggets have been signaling for months that they …
item.description: The same most certainly goes for Denver and Peyton Watson. The Nuggets have been signaling for months that they plan to match any offer sheet for Watson … to the point that sources say it would take an offer sheet in the $30 million range in order for Denver to balk at re-signing him. The Lakers and Bulls are two teams that have been mentioned as potential Watson suitors going back to February's trade deadline. Sources with knowledge of the situation have this week added that the Clippers are al

item.title: Timofey Mozgov on 2016 Cavs reunion: 'No one called me'
item.description: Hosted by LeBron James, the luxury multi-country vacation featured core members of the historic 2015–16 roster, including Kevin Love, J.R. Smith, Richard Jefferson, Channing Frye, and Tristan Thompson, with Iman Shumpert and Matthew Dellavedova joining the group later. Despite playing in 13 games during that legendary postseason run, the 39-year-old Russian big was completely omitted from the planning. Speaking with the Russian outlet Sport Express on Friday, Timofey Mozgov revealed that nobody

item.title: Deandre Ayton expected to pick up his player option
item.description: It’s no secret the Lakers are looking to upgrade at center (it should be noted Deandre Ayton is expected to pick up his player option and be back with the team). Robinson is the kind of big man they could use, but his health concerns — particularly his chronic ankle issues — will give the Lakers and every team some pause.

item.title: Nets, Spurs interested in signing Rui Hachimura
item.description: Brooklyn keeps coming up as a likely suitor, too, for the Lakers' Rui Hachimura, league sources say. The Nets and Spurs are two teams we've heard have interest in adding Hachimura's services, while the Spurs are also faced with resolving Harrison Barnes' future as the veteran forward heads to unrestricted free agency.

item.title: LeBron was in a bad mood about ESPN. He thought ESPN …
item.description: Heat Central: LeBron James felt like ESPN took advantage of him in 2010 when he decided to join the Heat, per @WindhorstESPN “The Heat did not like us there. LeBron was in a bad mood about ESPN. He thought ESPN had turned his back on him and taken advantage of him in the wake of the decision. He was annoyed by all the coverage. Looking back on it, it was annoying.”

item.title: Tyler Herro drew trade interest from Lakers and Nets before Bucks trade
item.description: Before Herro joined Milwaukee in the Antetokounmpo trade and the Los Angeles Lakers agreed to a four-year, $185 million max deal with Austin Reaves, the Lakers had trade discussions surrounding Herro and Kel’el Ware, which would’ve involved multiple first-round draft picks, league sources told HoopsHype. In addition, the Brooklyn Nets also did due diligence and gauged the price tag for Herro, HoopsHype has learned.

item.title: Cam carr’s dad chris carr confirmed that luka doncic …
item.description: Coach Rome: cam carr’s dad chris carr confirmed that luka doncic and austin reaves have already texted cam welcoming him to the lakers

item.title: Cam Johnson has drawn trade interest from Lakers, Celtics, Heat, Clippers, Magic
item.description: Meanwhile, as the Nuggets navigate getting below the second apron, sharpshooting forward Cam Johnson has drawn trade interest from several teams, including the Los Angeles Lakers, Boston Celtics, Miami Heat, Los Angeles Clippers, and Orlando Magic, league sources told HoopsHype. The 30-year-old forward shot career-highs from the field (.480) and beyond the arc (.430), and is on an expiring $23.06 million contract for the upcoming season.

item.title: Cavs, 76eres, Lakers, Spurs, Suns, Pistons, Blazers among teams interested in Dean Wade?
item.description: Cleveland Cavaliers forward Dean Wade is expected to have various suitors in free agency, including the Cavaliers, Philadelphia 76ers, Los Angeles Lakers, San Antonio Spurs, Phoenix Suns, Detroit Pistons, and Portland Trail Blazers, league sources told HoopsHype.

item.title: Landale, 30, is expected to have a strong market, …
item.description: Landale, 30, is expected to have a strong market, including interest from the Hawks, Chicago Bulls, Los Angeles Lakers, Los Angeles Clippers, Philadelphia 76ers, and Cleveland Cavaliers, league sources told HoopsHype. Expect the Australian center to command offers north of the bi-annual exception in free agency.

item.title: Austin Reaves was going to get paid and he's got …
item.description: Law Murray: "Free agency is really difficult and these teams talk so much. That's why we have the trash rumor mill that we have now. Austin Reaves was going to get paid and he's got really good representation. These dudes have a feel and the pulse of the league and he offers an ability that, if he would have left, he would have been a difference-maker wherever he would go. I've got a lot of respect for Austin Reaves' game and so do a lot of teams in the league."

item.title: "Joel wanted (LeBron James) on the Sixers, okay, back …
item.description: Law Murray: "Joel wanted (LeBron James) on the Sixers, okay, back in 2018. Like, really, really bad. And when LeBron left Cleveland for the Lakers, Joel had one of my favorite of his troll tweets when he was like, I'm paraphrasing, but he was like, they're not going to like you in Los Angeles. That's Kobe's town. And that dude LeBron, it's been that's how it's felt. Like he's been polarizing in a Los Angeles uniform because you got Bron fans, you got Lakers fans, you got Kobe fans. It's a whole

item.title: Lakers have tendered a two-way qualifying offer to …
item.description: Keith Smith: The Los Angeles Lakers have tendered a two-way qualifying offer to Chris Manon, a league source told @spotrac. Manon will now be a restricted free agent this summer.

item.title: Whether Bronny is a professional at that level yet is …
item.description: Karlo Matkovic on LeBron James: ‘He literally made a son to be his teammate. People talk about him and say he’s not good, but at the very least, he brought his son into the league. LeBron said he can play, he said '100% believe me.' And Bronny is not bad in the G League; he is strong, even though he is small. You have people like Brunson saying from the side that he thinks he can't make it, but all those haters... everyone has their own opinion. It's a unique situation in the league. You have to

item.title: Vladimir Radmanovic after forcing Lakers trade to Bobcats: 'Be careful what you wish for'
item.description: Vladimir Radmanovic on being traded to Hornets: ‘After being kept on the bench for nearly two months with only 'teaspoon' minutes, I couldn't establish any continuity as a player. It was one of the most frustrating periods of my career. I eventually went to Mitch Kupchak and told him, 'If this is going to continue, please count me out and look for a trade.' It’s a classic case of 'be careful what you wish for.' I was traded to the Charlotte Bobcats, which was a franchise in total disarray at the

item.title: Vladimir Radmanovic: Kobe Bryant was like a shark, if he sensed a bit of blood, it was over
item.description: Vladimir Radmanovic: 'Kobe Bryant was like a shark; if he sensed even a little bit of blood, it was over. He tested every single teammate to see what he could expect from them in terms of mental toughness. This involved provocations, playing dirty during practice, and even throwing 'cheap shots.' To an outsider, it might look like something was wrong with him or that he wasn't balanced, but from the inside, you realized it was a constant test. I saw players like Smush Parker fail that test. Ther

item.title: I knew that joining the Lakers might not be the best …
item.description: Vladimir Radmanovic on being recruited by the Lakers: ‘When I was a free agent deciding where to sign, my manager told me that three people were going to contact me: Kobe Bryant, Magic Johnson, and Phil Jackson. No matter how humble you try to be, it is incredibly flattering when those three individuals call you personally and tell you they want you in their organization. Vlade Divac acted as a mediator, likely sharing my phone number. I knew that joining the Lakers might not be the best option

item.title: Vladimir Radmanovic on the Influence of Phil Jackson’s …
item.description: Vladimir Radmanovic on the Influence of Phil Jackson’s methods: ‘Phil Jackson was an incredibly interesting type of coach, unlike anyone else in the league. He had these methods—meditation, yoga, and turning off the lights in the locker room. He would tell us to close our eyes and 'think of nothing.' At the time, I was just starting to explore my faith more seriously, and I felt like meditating didn't really fit with my beliefs, so I’d just use the time to take a quick nap while he talked. But h

item.title: LeBron James has not expressed interest in returning to Heat yet
item.description: Speculation continues to swirl about whether LeBron James could have interest in a return to the Heat. Nothing should be ruled out, but this is not something that has been actively pursued by James to this point, according to a source. Miami very likely would explore if James decides to pursue it.

item.title: Warriors trying to trade for Anthony Davis to entice LeBron James to join
item.description: The Warriors are attempting to trade for Wizards big Anthony Davis and then sign Lakers free agent forward LeBron James, according to multiple league sources.

item.title: Sources say the Warriors hope adding Davis would help …
item.description: Sources say the Warriors hope adding Davis would help lure LeBron from Los Angeles to the Bay Area once free agency officially opens on June 30. The pitch would be simple: reunite with AD, team up with Steph Curry and Draymond Green, play for Steve Kerr, and chase one more championship with a roster of legends.

item.title: "The priorities for the Pistons this summer is to …
item.description: Marc Stein: "The priorities for the Pistons this summer is to re-sign Duren and to do an extension with Ausar Thompson. And we haven't even talked about restricted free agency that there are some really good restricted free agents. Duren, your guy Peyton Watson, Walker Kessler, who of course we know the Lakers would love to try to acquire if possible."

item.title: Lakers eyeing Ziaire Williams
item.description: According to a league source, one player the Lakers could take a look at in free agency is Brooklyn small forward Zaire Williams, who had his team option declined by the Nets on Sunday. The Lakers have desires to get younger and more athletic on the wing, and Williams is coming off back-to-back seasons in Brooklyn where he averaged more than 10 points and shot better than 34 percent from 3-point range.

item.title: Byron Scott says everybody thinks it’s LeBron James …
item.description: NBA Courtside: Byron Scott says everybody thinks it’s LeBron James decision to stay at the Lakers, but the organization should say we’re moving on: “We’re gonna start looking at other options to bring in to make us a better organization.” (Via Byron Scott’s Fast Break)

item.title: Byron Scott says LeBron James is not a top 5 All-Time …
item.description: Byron Scott says LeBron James is not a top 5 All-Time Lakers player, he might be top 10: “I’m going by if you put that purple and gold there’s a different standard. So, I’m going to elaborate on a little bit. There’s a different standard and you look at the top guys, the Kareem, the Magic’s, the Kobe’s, the Shaq’s, you know, guys like that. Those guys got multiple championships with this organization.”

item.title: LeBron James has played with or against 37.1% of all …
item.description: Crazy Stats: LeBron James has played with or against 37.1% of all @NBA players in history, that includes 200+ opponents from 4 different decades of draft classes (pre-2000, 2001-10, 2011-20 and 2021-current). Moreover, James appeared in 28.8% of all NBA seasons.

item.title: Windhorst: LeBron James has not made himself available to meet with the Lakers yet
item.description: Heat Central: LeBron James HAS NOT made himself available to meet with the Lakers, per @WindhorstESPN 👀 “They reached out to LeBron representative Rich Paul and said ‘we’d like to have LeBron Back.’ But there have been no further talks. From what I understand, the Lakers have not made an offer. But LeBron, from what I understand as of today, when I got this update, LeBron has not, you know, made himself available to meet with the Lakers yet.”

item.title: Two of the most valuable rookie cards for Shohei …
item.description: Two of the most valuable rookie cards for Shohei Ohtani and LeBron James sold through Goldin Auctions on Sunday night, as money continues to flood into the sports card market. Ohtani’s 2018 Topps Chrome #150 SuperFractor, the one-of-a-kind parallel of one of his top rookie cards, sold for $2.56 million, including the buyer’s premium. This exact card last sold in 2022 for $139,200. It is now the second highest publicly known sale of an Ohtani trading card behind his one-of-one 2025 Topps Chrome G

item.title: “The notion of them trying to trade for AD first as a …
item.description: To sweeten the deal, journalist Kevin O’Connor reported that Davis could also be lured to Dub Nation. However, NBA insider Marc Stein was quick to pump the brakes on those reports, explaining why such a blockbuster move is far from materializing. “Based on everything I was told tonight, I don’t think there is anything active there,” Marc Stein stated on The All-NBA Podcast. “The notion of them trying to trade for AD first as a lure for LeBron, hard to see that happening in that sequence… I think

item.title: They can re-sign Tari Eason and offer Marcus Smart a …
item.description: ‪Yossi Gozlan‬: Houston Rockets salary cap situation with Fred VanVleet opting in. They can re-sign Tari Eason and offer Marcus Smart a significant portion of their non-tax mid-level exception while remaining under the first apron if they clear money first, such as Dorian Finney-Smith.

item.title: Draymond Green's opt-out gives Warriors room to pursue Anthony Davis and recruit LeBron James
item.description: Shams Charania: The opt-out comes days after the Warriors said they expect Green to pick up the $27.7M option. Instead Green delivers Golden State time and space to pursue Davis with the Wizards and convince James to join Stephen Curry, Green and his old Lakers championship teammate in the Bay.

item.title: “This is potentially a very big domino in this NBA …
item.description: Brian Windhorst on what Draymond opting out means for LeBron: “This is potentially a very big domino in this NBA offseason… This opens the door for the Warriors to use their MLE with $15M… The Lakers want to meet with him and give him their plan but the way it was explained to me, LeBron has been unavailable for a meeting with the Lakers… I would just say this, stay tuned.”

item.title: They've got $48 million below the first apron to …
item.description: ‪Yossi Gozlan‬: Golden State Warriors salary cap situation after Draymond Green opted out, clearing $27.7 million off their payroll. They've got $48 million below the first apron to budget re-signing him, Kristaps Porzingis, and LeBron James with the $15 million non-tax mid-level exception.

item.title: Draymond Green declines player option
item.description: Shams Charania: Golden State Warriors forward Draymond Green is declining his $27.7 million player option to become a free agent, sources tell ESPN. This move gives the Warriors flexibility to pursue LeBron James in free agency and Anthony Davis via trade to form a Big 4.

item.title: The Warriors have A LOT more flexibility now for …
item.description: Kevin O'Connor: This is a huge development. The Warriors have A LOT more flexibility now for pursuing LeBron James AND Anthony Davis, which as I reported last night is their plan.

item.title: Deandre Ayton to opt into player option for 2026-27 season to return to Lakers
item.description: Shams Charania: Los Angeles Lakers center Deandre Ayton is opting into his $8.1 million player option for the 2026-27 season, sources tell ESPN.

item.title: LeBron James, Lakers have yet to discuss future, but that's expected to change following Warriors reports
item.description: Chris Haynes: LeBron James and Los Angeles Lakers still have yet to have a conversation regarding his future but that’s expected to change in light of Golden State Warriors’ strategic maneuvering to position themselves as a landing spot, sources tell me.

item.title: Marcus Smart declines player option and will enter unrestricted free agency
item.description: Chris Haynes: Los Angeles Lakers guard Marcus Smart is declining his $5.4M player option to become an unrestricted free agent, league sources tell me.

item.title: Los Angeles Lakers guard Marcus Smart will decline his …
item.description: Michael Scotto: JUST IN: Los Angeles Lakers guard Marcus Smart will decline his $5.39 million player option for the 2026-27 season and enter unrestricted free agency, league sources told @hoopshype . Smart started 54 of 62 games played last season for the Lakers. He’ll be coveted in free agency.

item.title: LeBron James prioritizing happiness over money in free agency?
item.description: Brian Windhorst: "It's been communicated to me that the number one thing for LeBron (James) next season is happiness, not money. So this is obviously we knew that he's going to have to take a pay cut from 50 million. So that has been communicated to me. And what does that mean? I don't know. Does that mean he's willing to take the mid-level? Does that mean he's willing to take the taxpayer mid-level? The mid-level is about 15, the taxpayer mid-level is like six and a half or seven or something l

item.title: Rich Paul met with Wizards to discuss Anthony Davis' future with the franchise
item.description: Anthony Davis' agent, Rich Paul, met with the Washington Wizards on Sunday to discuss his future with the franchise. Davis is eligible this offseason for a four-year extension worth up to $275 million this offseason with the Wizards. As the Wizards recently signed Trae Young to a four-year, $212 million contract, Davis is clearly gauging whether he could receive a similar commitment from the franchise. Davis has also been the subject of trade rumors involving the Golden State Warriors with a pot

item.title: "I'm sure that Anthony Davis could be given a contract …
item.description: "Anthony Davis just saw Trae Young get a $200 million new contract with the Wizards," said ESPN's Brian Windhorst on Monday's episode of Get Up. "Yesterday, Rich Paul, who represents Draymond Green and LeBron and Anthony Davis met with the Wizards to discuss the future. "I'm sure that Anthony Davis could be given a contract that would make him very happy to be in Washington. Maybe the Wizards are prepared to do that. The Wizards want to win immediately. "If not, the Warriors could potentially co

item.title: Marcus Smart is now officially declining his $5.4 …
item.description: Marc Stein: Marcus Smart is now officially declining his $5.4 million player option with the Lakers for next season amid a growing expectation Houston is eager to reunite Smart and Ime Udoka once free agency begins Tuesday at 6 PM ET.

item.title: Windhorst: LeBron James has not been available to meet with Lakers
item.description: Lakers Daily: REPORT: LeBron James has "not been available" for a meeting with the Lakers "LeBron has just not been available for the meeting. … I don’t know why LeBron has not been available, but he has not been available for the meeting.” (via @WindhorstESPN)

item.title: Likely prohibits Golden State from using the full …
item.description: Shams Charania: The Warriors were eligible to reach an extension with Porzingis ahead of the veteran center hitting the open market on Tuesday night. The deal, according to @BobbyMarks42 , likely prohibits Golden State from using the full non-taxpayer mid-level exception.

item.title: They would presumably swap Jimmy Butler and picks for …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: Warriors cap situation after extending Kristaps Porzingis and De'Anthony Melton presumably opting out. The LeBron James-Anthony Davis pipedream is still alive. They would presumably swap Jimmy Butler and picks for Davis. Then they could fit James with $15 million non-taxpayer mid-level under the first apron hard cap IF Draymond Green takes the same contract as Porzingis, and IF they also find a taker for Moses Moody.

item.title: Pistons uninterested in pursuing sign-and-trade scenarios for Jalen Duren
item.description: Dave McMenamin: Detroit Pistons restricted free agent center Jalen Duren has a planned call with the Los Angeles Lakers on Tuesday, a league source confirmed to ESPN ( @sam_amick first). However DET thus far is uninterested in pursuing a sign and trade with their All-Star big man, sources said.

item.title: No tangible movement for Anthony Davis to Golden State talks
item.description: That could also mean exploring a trade for Washington center Anthony Davis to help lure James, though there has yet to be tangible movement on that front, league sources said.

item.title: Lakers expected to call Jalen Duren when free agency opens
item.description: Sam Amick: More intel on Jalen Duren coming in: League sources tell @TheAthletic he now has a call with the Lakers scheduled for tomorrow. Another sign-and-trade scenario emerges…

item.title: Lakes decline team option on Nick Smith Jr
item.description: Keith Smith: The Los Angeles Lakers declined their team option for Nick Smith Jr., a league source told @spotrac . Smith will be an unrestricted free agent this offseason.

item.title: Unless LeBron wants to come on the minimum, no, you …
item.description: Bobby Marks: "I never rule anything out in this world, but you just look at the math of it. They are 27 million below the first apron. They are going to bring back Draymond Green. You don't leave 27 on the table to explore free agency. Even if he came back at 20 million, you are 7 million, and anything you use more than 6.1 of that non-tax mid-level, you get hard-capped. So when you look at it from that perspective, unless LeBron wants to come on the minimum, no, you don't have 15 million to off

item.title: Some rival NBA executives and coaches have mixed …
item.description: The Lakers still have to prove they can maximize Dončić’s championship window. But Dončić, Reaves and James showed they can still thrive together even amid the discomfort when one of them played off the ball. In this case, mostly James. Some rival NBA executives and coaches have mixed feelings on whether James has the appetite to repeat that experience again. Some remain bullish on James’ versatility and unselfishness. Some express skepticism on whether James can stay patient with Dončić b

item.title: This is going end up with Draymond getting a new …
item.description: [Windhorst] "The people on the league I'm talking to today are giving me the side eye and they're going, this is going end up with Draymond getting a new contract in GS, LeBron getting a new contract in LA and AD getting a new contract in Washington."

item.title: Yaxel said his friends spent all day texting him and …
item.description: Nick Friedell: Yaxel said his friends spent all day texting him and asking how he would fit in playing with Steph, Draymond, LeBron and AD.

item.title: The Lakers, for their part, are saying almost nothing. …
item.description: The Lakers, for their part, are saying almost nothing. No one has offered real hints, according to league sources, about the plans for LeBron James — even to players currently on the roster.

item.title: Kings, Lakers both in the mix for Jalen Duren
item.description: The Jalen Duren situation is getting more interesting by the day. As we first reported Friday, the third-team All-NBA big man was underwhelmed by Detroit’s offer in restricted free agency and almost immediately began strategizing about sign-and-trade scenarios out of town. And as we learned Monday afternoon via league sources, the Sacramento Kings and Los Angeles Lakers are now officially on that list.

item.title: The Kings and Lakers are scheduled to meet with Duren …
item.description: Those sources, who are not authorized to discuss free-agent scenarios before the offseason officially begins, say the Kings and Lakers are scheduled to meet with Duren after free agency opens Tuesday. It’s unclear whether the Kings’ visit will be in person or virtual, but those sources say the Lakers’ meeting is expected to be by phone.

item.title: Luka Doncic a big fan of Jalen Duren's game
item.description: (The Lakers’ incumbent starting center, Deandre Ayton, picked up his $8.1 million player option Monday.) Yet in stark contrast to the Kings’ scenario, the Lakers’ best offerings might be draft capital rather than quality players, as Detroit could trade Duren into the Lakers’ salary-cap space. Lakers star Luka Dončić is known to be a fan of Duren’s game, league sources say, which is a monumental factor for any player who’s trying to be welcomed into Crypto.com Arena these days.

item.title: Luka Doncic for his desire to pair with an "A-list …
item.description: Detroit Pistons center Jalen Duren, one of the top restricted free agents this summer, has a pair of meetings with outside teams scheduled for when free agency officially begins Tuesday. One of the teams is the Los Angeles Lakers, a source familiar with the situation told ESPN. L.A.'s front office has been given feedback from franchise star Luka Doncic for his desire to pair with an "A-list center," a league source told ESPN, and Duren would certainly fit the bill. The other team Duren plans to

item.title: Lakers eyeing Sandro Mamukelashvili
item.description: But people around the NBA, tasked with trying to make sense of the evolving free-agency landscape, started to link the Lakers to a wide range of players throughout Monday. One name has come up from multiple league sources: Toronto forward/center Sandro Mamukelashvili. Mamukelashvili declined his $2.8 million option with the Raptors Monday, with league sources believing he could command $10 million or more in free agency.

item.title: Anthony Davis to Warriors 'unlikely'
item.description: Brian Windhorst: The Warriors still have the ability to re-sign Draymond Green to a reasonable contract number and to try to add LeBron James, but it's really, really narrow and they would really need to make some other moves to build up their team. Right now, I'm taking the Anthony Davis thing off the front burner. I do not have data that says that that's likely to happen.

item.title: Bronny James' deal with Lakers becomes fully guaranteed
item.description: Keith Smith: Bronny James' contract with the Los Angeles Lakers became fully guaranteed for $2.3M yesterday.

item.title: Lakers had meeting with Luka Doncic last summer mapping out 2026 offseason; plans did not include LeBron James
item.description: SM Highlights: The Lakers Had A Meeting Last Summer With Luka Doncic That Mapped Out The 2026 Offseason, LeBron James WASN’T In Those Plans. Per, @ramonashelburne “At that meeting they laid out their plans… in those plans, they did NOT include LeBron James.

item.title: Draymond Green on Austin Reaves' new contract: I didn't expect the Lakers to go so high
item.description: Draymond Green: I just didn’t expect the Lakers to go so high. On a team where you had Austin Reaves, you had Luka Doncic, and you had LeBron James, the team didn’t fare as well as one would hope. So, I thought the Lakers would stand pat. A little bit longer maybe try to see if they can negotiate that number down quite a bit, but apparently the Pistons raise the offer.

item.title: Suns have a strong level of interest in Luke Kennard
item.description: Grant Afseth: The Phoenix Suns have a strong level of interest in Luke Kennard ahead of NBA free agency. Kennard averaged 8.4 points, 2.3 rebounds, and 2.2 assists last season, while shooting 47.8% from 3-point range.

item.title: LeBron James informs Lakers they can move on without him because he will continue career elsewhere
item.description: Shams Charania: BREAKING: LeBron James will continue his NBA career for the 2026-27 season and has informed the Los Angeles Lakers that the franchise can move on without him because he will play elsewhere, Klutch Sports CEO Rich Paul tells ESPN.

item.title: Lakers told LeBron James they wanted him back, but he decided to move on himself: 'It's time to move on'
item.description: Shams Charania: The Lakers expressed to James that they wanted him back, but the NBA's all-time leading scorer decides to move on elsewhere.

item.title: A source close to LeBron James told ESPN, “it was time …
item.description: Dave McMenamin: A source close to LeBron James told ESPN, “it was time to move on.”

item.title: LeBron James has informed the Los Angeles Lakers that …
item.description: Chris Haynes: BREAKING: LeBron James has informed the Los Angeles Lakers that he will play elsewhere for the 2026-27 season, his agent Rich Paul tells me.

item.title: After eight seasons with the Lakers, including leading …
item.description: Shams Charania: After eight seasons with the Lakers, including leading the franchise to the 2020 NBA championship, James departs and enters free agency set to join a new team. Lakers president Rob Pelinka and Rich Paul spoke a short time ago about the four-time champion's plans, sources said. James chose to inform the Lakers well before start of free agency tonight out of courtesy and appreciation for their run together, and to allow LA to conduct its offseason business.

item.title: LeBron James has informed the Lakers he is leaving and …
item.description: Dan Woike: LeBron James has informed the Lakers he is leaving and will be playing elsewhere next season, sources close to James say. He has not yet decided where he’ll be playing next season and felt it was time to move on.

item.title: Warriors 'at the front of the line' in pursuit of LeBron James
item.description: Marc Stein: The Warriors are at the front of the line in pursuit of LeBron James in free agency and league sources tell @TheSteinLine that Golden State is not operating as though it has a mandate to also pursue an Anthony Davis trade.

item.title: Cavaliers have interest in second reunion with LeBron James
item.description: Chris Haynes: Cleveland Cavaliers have interest in a second reunion with LeBron James with the appeal of the franchise’s greatest player finishing his career where it all started, league sources tell me.

item.title: There was nothing percolating on LeBron front for …
item.description: Barry Jackson: There was nothing percolating on LeBron front for Miami when the Heat opted not to create full mid-level money in recent days. They could create full $15.1 mid-level money, if LeBron somewhat surprisingly decides to leave the West and come here, by attaching a draft pick to Jovic with hope of finding a taker.

item.title: LeBron James to Warriors not a done deal yet
item.description: Anthony Slater: Warriors decision makers have been given no indication at this hour that they are the firm LeBron James landing spot, per sources. There's a belief that they'll have plenty of appeal, but appears to be some level of recruitment required.

item.title: Golden State will certainly be pitching LeBron James …
item.description: Jake Fischer: Golden State will certainly be pitching LeBron James to join Stephen Curry and Draymond Green with the Warriors. From last night: "Golden State is known to be the league's most interested external suitor for the 41-year-old."

item.title: LeBron James to Miami has been discussed around the league as a possibility
item.description: Heat Central: REPORT: LeBron James has been linked to the Miami Heat after the Giannis Antetokounmpo trade, per @JakeLFischer “He’s been connected to Miami, a potential homecoming there once Giannis was traded to South Beach. There’s plenty talk around the league that they could see that reunion”

item.title: Cavaliers, Heat, Warriors expected to emerge as suitors for LeBron James
item.description: SportsCenter: I'm told this is a happiness-led decision for LeBron James. ... Wherever he lands in free agency, it will not be driven by money." "It's unclear what the timeline will be. Right now, he's in Ohio spending time with his family. He's gonna make a decision on his own time. But there's no question all of the NBA's contending teams are in the picture for LeBron James. Rich Paul will call LeBron with expressed interest as soon as free agency opens up tonight. Around the league, teams exp

item.title: “You look at teams he’s been too, you look at the …
item.description: Heat Central: “You look at teams he’s been too, you look at the Cavs, and you look at the Heat. He’s been in those places. He knows the terrain. I think it’s too soon to say anyone is the front runner” — @TheSteinLine on LeBron destinations

item.title: LeBron James, Luka Doncic had no personal issues with each other; James departure unrelated to Doncic
item.description: There might be an instinct here to think that Luka Doncic helped push LeBron James out the door, but league sources who know both, who were granted anonymity because they were not authorized to discuss the situation, insist the two players had no personal issues with one another. James, after all, went to the Lakers, and said Dončić should have his name introduced last in the starting lineups. And this summer, LeBron and Luka shared text messages about golf, a hobby they've both picked up in t

item.title: Thank you, LeBron 💜💛
item.description: Los Angeles Lakers: Thank you, LeBron 💜💛

item.title: The Lakers are expected to target Jalen Duren, Walker …
item.description: NBA Central: The Lakers are expected to target Jalen Duren, Walker Kessler, Peyton Watson, Mitchell Robinson, Sandro Mamukelashvili, Luke Kennard, and Rui Hachimura, per @ramonashelburne

item.title: Cavaliers open to the idea of a LeBron James return
item.description: Sources tell cleveland.com that the Cavs are open to the idea of a LeBron James return. Of course, signing the franchise legend for one final stint isn’t exactly straightforward given their salary cap situation. Although cap-strapped Cleveland is currently $29.1 million below the first apron and $42.1 million below the second apron, those numbers don’t reflect the expected multiyear deal James Harden is expected to sign in the near future.

item.title: Luka Doncic says goodbye to LeBron James: 'An honor to play with and learn from you'
item.description: Law Murray: From Luka Doncic to LeBron James

item.title: Magic Johnson: I want to thank LeBron James and his …
item.description: Magic Johnson: I want to thank LeBron James and his super agent Rich Paul for taking a meeting with me back in 2018 when I was President of the Lakers and deciding to sign with us. He created a whole new era for the Lakers, including leading us to an NBA Championship in 2020 and all the milestones he surpassed in a Lakers jersey. I’ll always love and be grateful for him and wish him all the best in the next phase of his life, on and off the court!

item.title: LeBron James to talk to several teams after free agency opens
item.description: Ramona Shelburne: LeBron James and/or his representatives will talk to a few teams after free agency opens in a few hours. A source close to the process says there's “no rush" for him to make a decision.

item.title: LeBron James on social media: Truly a honor to wear the purple and gold
item.description: LeBron James: No, THANK YOU! Truly a honor to wear the 💜💛 while trying to continuing the greatness & legacies that came before me! Hope I made a few proud during my stint. 🙏🏾🫡👑

item.title: Lakers interested in Quentin Grimes
item.description: Marc Stein: Last night we reported the Lakers' rising interest in Toronto free agent Sandro Mamukelashvili. Now league sources tell @JakeLFischer and me that Philadelphia's Quentin Grimes is emerging as another top Lakers target. MORE Intel here: tinyurl.com/bde3f33k

item.title: @clevelanddotcom that Cleveland has remained engaged …
item.description: Chris Fedor: Despite the LeBron James news and #Cavs being interested, sources tell @clevelanddotcom that Cleveland has remained engaged in conversations with free agent Dean Wade about the possibility of him returning.

item.title: Lakers could potentially sign both Sandro …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: The Lakers could potentially sign both Sandro Mamukelashvili & Quentin Grimes while preserving a $41.25 million max slot. One could go into their surplus cap space (~$11 million, could create more via trade). The other could go into the $9.4 million room mid-level.

item.title: LeBron was a winner on and off the court, and Laker …
item.description: Kareem Abdul-Jabbar: @KingJames LeBron was a winner on and off the court, and Laker fan should appreciate his performance for the last 8 years. If LeBron goes home to Cleveland, it won’t matter if they win the world championship or not because it’s Hometown fans will get their opportunity to say their goodbyes to one of the greatest ever. And who knows one day he may become Mayor!! @Lakers @NBAcom

item.title: The 3 teams I mentioned (Heat, GSW, CLE) you can …
item.description: Heat Central: LeBron James main priority is going to a team that has a chance at winning, per @ChrisBHaynes "I'm told he's going to be open minded and patient for his next team.... The one component a team is going to have to have to secure his service from what i'm told, is the team has to be competitive and have a realistic chance at winning.... The 3 teams I mentioned (Heat, GSW, CLE) you can pretty much look and see of those 3 who has the best shot at winning... Money wasn't mentioned"

item.title: All of that, according to multiple NBA sources, is …
item.description: Is LeBron’s interest contingent on the Warriors acquiring his old Lakers teammate and fellow Paul client Anthony Davis, which would take the Warriors putting Jimmy Butler into any offer with the Wizards for salary-matching purposes? And would the Warriors have to commit to a long-term extension with Davis as part of this mammoth undertaking? All of that, according to multiple NBA sources, is mostly being assumed but has not been explicitly demanded by LeBron or Paul. And the Davis/Butler part of

item.title: But also, what better way for Paul to shake loose the …
item.description: Many league sources have long speculated that if and when LeBron and the Lakers soured on each other, the Warriors would be LeBron’s first choice, if he wanted to continue playing and certain conditions could be met. And I think Monday’s intrigue was an indication that he wants to continue playing, let’s put it that way. But also, what better way for Paul to shake loose the Lakers’ best offer than whispering that LeBron is extremely interested in wandering a bit north and joining up with Curry a

item.title: Utah Jazz center and restricted free agent Walker …
item.description: Utah Jazz center and restricted free agent Walker Kessler will reportedly meet with the Los Angeles Lakers as the NBA’s free agency window opens. Kessler was spotted arriving in Los Angeles ahead of his meeting with the Lakers’ brass. “I’m here,” Kessler said in a video published by TMZ Sports. The Lakers have more money to play with than previously expected. On Tuesday, it was announced that LeBron James would leave L.A. and join another team. That makes the Lakers — already in need of a center

item.title: “I did see that,” Kessler said when asked about James’ …
item.description: “I did see that,” Kessler said when asked about James’ decision to leave the Lakers on Tuesday. “Obviously, he’s one of the greatest players of all time, so he would have been great to play with, but we just gotta see what happens, man.”

item.title: LeBron was a winner on and off the court, and Laker …
item.description: Kareem Abdul-Jabbar: @KingJames LeBron was a winner on and off the court, and Laker fans should appreciate his performance for the last 8 years. If LeBron goes home to Cleveland, it won’t matter if they win the World Championship or not because his hometown fans will get their opportunity to say their goodbyes to one of the greatest ever. And who knows one day he may become Mayor of Cleveland !!

item.title: “They (warriors) are at the front of the line, but …
item.description: Heat Central: The Miami Heat have interest in LeBron James, per @TheSteinLine “They (warriors) are at the front of the line, but there is a line…. Cleveland is certainly going to try and convince him of a 3rd marriage, Miami has interest…. This might not necessarily happen tonight”

item.title: Shams: A dozen teams expected to pitch LeBron James
item.description: Fullcourtpass: There is expected to be a DOZEN teams that pitch LeBron James and Rich Paul is already listening to offers, per @ShamsCharania
